# Anubee v0.1.0

First public release. One static aarch64 binary; no install, no dependencies.

`arm64` · `eBPF` · `root required`

## Compatibility

| Requirement | Detail |
|---|---|
| Architecture | arm64 / aarch64 only |
| OS / kernel | **Android 12+ / GKI 5.10 or newer** (needs BTF + BPF ring buffer) |
| Root | required (`su -c`, or `adb root`) |
| SELinux | usually must be **permissive** to load eBPF |
| Tested on | Android 15 / GKI 6.6 (POCO C85) |

Non-GKI or vendor kernels without `/sys/kernel/btf/vmlinux`, and Android 11 or
older (kernel 5.4, no ring buffer), are not supported.

## Capabilities

| Subcommand | What it's for |
|---|---|
| `anubee syscalls` | Watches what an app does without it ever knowing, the quiet default |
| `anubee funcs` | Captures deep function-level detail: arguments, return values, and timing |
| `anubee lib` | Finds every native library an app loads, even ones it only loads mid-run |
| `anubee dump` | Pulls packed or encrypted code out of a running app and rebuilds it into a file you can open in a disassembler |
| `anubee correlate` | Ties a specific function to the syscalls it triggers |
| `anubee trace` | Runs `syscalls` and `funcs`/`lib` together in one launch |
| `anubee mod` | Runs ready-made analyzers for specific malware behavior |

## Quick start

Download `anubee-v0.1.0`, push it to a rooted device, run your first trace.
Full walkthrough and prerequisites: [`docs/getting-started.md`](https://github.com/michaelaurelio/Anubee/blob/main/docs/getting-started.md).

## Verify your download

```
sha256sum anubee-v0.1.0
```

Expected:

```
__SHA256__  anubee-v0.1.0
```

## Known limitations

- Root is required; SELinux commonly must be permissive to load eBPF, and a RASP
  may itself treat permissive mode as a tamper signal.
- Non-GKI / no-BTF kernels are unsupported without regenerating `vmlinux.h`.
- Loud capabilities (`funcs`, `correlate`, `trace`) write a uprobe into the
  target and are detectable by design; the quiet ones (`syscalls`, `lib`,
  `dump`) are not.

## Build from source

See the build section of [`docs/getting-started.md`](https://github.com/michaelaurelio/Anubee/blob/main/docs/getting-started.md).
